# fom.fem_utils

Utilities for finite element (FE) work—especially handy for preconditioners, mesh, and nonlinear solvers.

**Summary:**  
Provides functions for mesh/basis handling, fast solvers, region splitting, domain helpers, and attribute cleanup.  
Perfect for making FE codes faster, cleaner, and easier to use.

**Author:**  
Suparno Bhattacharyya

---

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [build_pc_amgsa](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.build_pc_amgsa) | Build a fast algebraic multigrid (AMG) preconditioner. |
| [compute_basis_regions](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.compute_basis_regions) | Make basis functions limited to mesh regions. |
| [element2location](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.element2location) | Get coordinates (locations) for elements in your mesh. |
| [load_domain](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.load_domain) | Load all info from `domain()` into your object. |
| [load_mesh_and_basis](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.load_mesh_and_basis) | Load just mesh and basis from domain info. |
| [newton_solver](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.newton_solver) | Newton nonlinear solver; uses PETSc if available. |
| [petsc_solve_csr](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.petsc_solve_csr) | Solve `A x = b` with PETSc KSP, using sparse (CSR) matrices. |
| [unwrap_attr](#skrom.fom.fem_utils.unwrap_attr) | Turn a 0-d object numpy array into its plain value. |

---

### build_pc_amgsa { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.build_pc_amgsa }

```python
fom.fem_utils.build_pc_amgsa(A, **kwargs)
```

Makes an algebraic multigrid (AMG) preconditioner—this helps large iterative solves (like CG) run much faster.

#### Parameters

| Name     | Type                              | Description                                   | Default    |
|----------|-----------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|------------|
| A        | scipy.sparse matrix or array_like | System matrix you want to precondition.       | _required_ |
| **kwargs | dict                              | Extra options for AMG solver.                 | `{}`       |

#### Returns

| Name   | Type                               | Description                                                               |
|--------|------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| M      | scipy.sparse.linalg.LinearOperator | The AMG preconditioner (as a linear operator for iterative solvers).      |

---

### compute_basis_regions { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.compute_basis_regions }

```python
fom.fem_utils.compute_basis_regions(basis, masks)
```

Splits your basis functions into regions defined by boolean masks (True/False arrays showing which elements belong to which region).

#### Parameters

| Name   | Type                           | Description                                                         | Default    |
|--------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| basis  | object                         | A basis object (has `nelems` and `with_elements` method).           | _required_ |
| masks  | dict of str to ndarray of bool | Maps region names to Boolean arrays for element selection.           | _required_ |

#### Returns

| Name         | Type                  | Description                                       |
|--------------|-----------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| region_bases | dict of str to object | Maps region names to basis objects for each region.|

---

### element2location { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.element2location }

```python
fom.fem_utils.element2location(mesh)
```

Gives the spatial coordinates for every element of the mesh (based on mesh connectivity).

#### Parameters

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                                                      | Default    |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| mesh   | object | Has `p` (coords, shape [spatial_dim, n_nodes]) and `t` (connectivity, shape [n_nodes_elem, n_elems]) | _required_ |

#### Returns

| Name           | Type                                         | Description                                                                      |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| element_coords | ndarray of shape (n_elements, n_local_nodes) | Each row: element coordinates (one coord per node, for each element).            |

---

### load_domain { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.load_domain }

```python
fom.fem_utils.load_domain(instance)
```

Calls your object’s `domain()` method and assigns all its outputs as attributes on the object.

#### Parameters

| Name     | Type   | Description                                                          | Default    |
|----------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| instance | object | Any object with a `domain()` method returning a dict of attributes.  | _required_ |

#### Notes

Sets fields on your instance in-place.  
Now you can use `instance.attr` to access domain attributes directly.

---

### load_mesh_and_basis { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.load_mesh_and_basis }

```python
fom.fem_utils.load_mesh_and_basis(instance)
```

Loads just `mesh` and `basis` fields from your object’s `domain()` output to the object’s attributes.

#### Parameters

| Name     | Type   | Description                                                             | Default    |
|----------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| instance | object | Object whose `domain()` returns at least 'mesh' and 'basis' keys.       | _required_ |

#### Notes

Only `mesh` and `basis` are loaded (rest are ignored).  
Instance is modified in-place.

---

### newton_solver { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.newton_solver }

```python
fom.fem_utils.newton_solver(
    assemble_fn,
    rhs_fn,
    u0,
    dirichlet_dofs=None,
    dirichlet_vals=None,
    *assemble_args,
    rhs_args=(),
    tol=0.01,
    maxit=50,
    alpha=1.0,
    jac_conditioner=False,
    ksp_type='cg',
    pc_type='ilu',
    ksp_rtol=1e-08,
    ksp_atol=0.0,
    ksp_max_it=2000,
    petsc_options=None,
    reuse_ksp=True,
    force_backend='auto',
)
```

Newton’s method nonlinear solver for systems of equations.  
Uses PETSc KSP for linear solves if PETSc is installed (unless you turn it off).  
Otherwise uses NumPy/Scipy solvers.

---

### petsc_solve_csr { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.petsc_solve_csr }

```python
fom.fem_utils.petsc_solve_csr(
    A_csr,
    b,
    *,
    ksp_type='cg',
    pc_type='ilu',
    rtol=1e-08,
    atol=0.0,
    max_it=2000,
    petsc_options=None,
    cache=None,
)
```

Solves `A x = b` using PETSc and Krylov solvers (good for large problems, uses cache for speed).  
Input matrix must be CSR format (from SciPy).

---

### unwrap_attr { #skrom.fom.fem_utils.unwrap_attr }

```python
fom.fem_utils.unwrap_attr(instance, attr_name)
```

If the named attribute is a 0-dimensional `numpy` object array, unwrap (flatten) it to the contained value.

#### Parameters

| Name      | Type   | Description                         | Default    |
|-----------|--------|-------------------------------------|------------|
| instance  | object | The object holding the attribute.   | _required_ |
| attr_name | str    | Attribute name (string).            | _required_ |

#### Notes

Only works if attribute is a 0-dim numpy array with object dtype.  
Instance is modified in-place—afterwards, the attribute is a plain value.
